content.problemStatement:
You are given a K√óKK \times KK√óK matrix MMM. For each rrr and ccc (1‚Č§r,c‚Č§K1 \le r, c \le K1‚Č§r,c‚Č§K), let's denote the element in the rrr-th row and ccc-th column by Mr,cM_{r, c}Mr,c‚Äč. You are also given three integers NNN, aaa and ddd. Let's define a K√óKK \times KK√óK matrix
S=‚ąĎi=0NFa+i‚čÖd‚čÖMi‚ÄČ.S = \sum_{i=0}^N F_{a + i \cdot d} \cdot M^i \,.S=‚ąĎi=0N‚ÄčFa+i‚čÖd‚Äč‚čÖMi.
Here, M0M^0M0 is the identity matrix and for any non-negative integer iii, FiF_iFi‚Äč is the iii-th Fibonacci number ‚Äē formally, F0=0F_0 = 0F0‚Äč=0, F1=1F_1 = 1F1‚Äč=1 and Fi=Fi‚ąí1+Fi‚ąí2F_i = F_{i-1} + F_{i-2}Fi‚Äč=Fi‚ąí1‚Äč+Fi‚ąí2‚Äč for i>1i \gt 1i>1.

Let's denote elements of the matrix SSS in the same way as for MMM. For each valid rrr and ccc, compute Sr,cS_{r, c}Sr,c‚Äč modulo 998,244,353998,244,353998,244,353.
constraints: 0‚Č§Mi,j<998,244,3530 \le M_{i, j} \lt 998,244,3530‚Č§Mi,j‚Äč<998,244,353 for each valid iii and jjj
the sum of KKK over all test cases does not exceed 100100100
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains four space-separated integers KKK, aaa, ddd and NNN.
KKK lines follow. For each valid iii, the iii-th of these lines contains KKK space-separated integers Mi,1,Mi,2,‚Ä¶,Mi,KM_{i, 1}, M_{i, 2}, \ldots, M_{i, K}Mi,1‚Äč,Mi,2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,Mi,K‚Äč.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print KKK lines. For each valid iii, the iii-th of these lines should contain KKK space-separated integers Si,1,Si,2,‚Ä¶,Si,KS_{i, 1}, S_{i, 2}, \ldots, S_{i, K}Si,1‚Äč,Si,2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,Si,K‚Äč modulo 998,244,353998,244,353998,244,353.
